checks.finding: REQ-001..REQ-004 each name literal terminal strings, exit codes, iteration counts, and byte-level checks (e.g. AC-001 'loop finished without reproducing (5 iterations)', AC-012/AC-015 zero-non-ASCII/no-BOM/no-CR). REQ-002's check-placeholders lock (AC-008/AC-009) asserts concrete exit 0 vs exit 1 on the same fixture. REQ-005's twin/degrade claim is measurable via AC-017's SKIP-with-reason requirement. REQ-006 resolves to concrete gates in AC-019 (validate-repository green, CHANGELOG entry citing issue numbers, no manual version bump). No in-scope requirement is left as unmeasurable intent.

Each Goal has direct AC coverage: REQ-001->AC-001..AC-006+AC-018; REQ-002->AC-007..AC-010+AC-018; REQ-003->AC-011..AC-013; REQ-004->AC-014..AC-016; REQ-005->AC-006/AC-010/AC-013/AC-016/AC-017; REQ-006->AC-019. acceptance-tests.md's Requirement column confirms the same mapping for all 19 ACs; no goal is left without a matching row.

ACs specify externally observable artifacts throughout: literal exit codes/strings (AC-001/AC-002), SHA-256 before/after equality on real files (AC-005, round-2 fix), byte-level ASCII/BOM/CR scans (AC-012/AC-015), and inventory-terminal-value equality across profiles (AC-010). The round-2 rewrite of AC-003/AC-005 against the real 5-state Audit-Status machine (Not-Started/Cycle-1-In-Progress/Cycle-2-In-Progress/Human-Pending/Human-Blocked, SKILL.md:34-43,61-65 per investigation INV-006) removes the prior state-machine mismatch and is now internally consistent with investigation.md INV-005..INV-007 (e.g. WFI-010.md's Audit-Attempt:1/Human-Pending pair is a valid permitted below-threshold state under the rewritten invariant).

Non-goals section is explicit and now includes the round-1-added directional limit: 'TEST-005 can only exercise the attempt < 3 direction on WFI-010.md/WFI-011.md... The Human-Blocked direction is covered by AC-003's synthetic sweep, not by the real-document smoke check.' Combined with existing exclusions (no loop-inventory re-registration, no live skill/gh invocation, no re-litigating #127, no protected-gate edits, tasks.md/traceability.md deferred to Phase 2, Pillar B/#125 out), the feature boundary is unambiguous for a downstream task author.

Security Boundaries table (B1-B4) states mktemp isolation, real-tree-never-written, and no-network guarantees. Edge Cases enumerate CI-resilience constraints with file:line citations (bash 3.2 empty-array handling INV-029, macOS TMPDIR pwd -P INV-030, Windows jq CRLF INV-031, real-validator Windows-CRLF fragility INV-032) plus the guard-ps1-ascii shared-file override-must-keep-working constraint and the exact-path landing constraint for both new .ps1 scripts. Roles and Permissions confirms none of the touched files are protected-gate (cited against sdd-hook-guard.py:886-927). No material constraint is left implicit.

Risks section names a Critical risk (skill-logic drift blessing a narrowed/widened cap) with mitigation mapped to AC-003's exact-line-cited reference check plus AC-005's real-document cross-check, and a High risk (mis-wired CHECK stub silently reporting false-positive) mitigated by AC-002 as a mandatory (not optional) canary. Medium/Low risks (shared guard-ps1-ascii file, loop-consistency.tests.sh edit, self-healing dependency) each cite a concrete AC or design.md precedent as validation surface. No high-risk claim is left without a planned check.
